title: Coil-built herb pot trio
subtitle: Three matching pots sized for small herb plants, built from coils
excerpt: Three small coil-built pots sized for 7 cm nursery pots or herb seedlings. Sealed air-dry clay holds a small terracotta insert for drainage, keeping the clay dry. Each pot takes about 100 g of clay and can be textured differently to create a coordinated but varied set.
sourceNotes: Coil-building technique derived from first principles.

content.text: : same height, same diameter, but each textured differently: pressed pattern, sgraffito line, plain smooth. Air-dry clay is not waterproof, so each pot holds a small terracotta or plastic nursery pot inside it. The clay outer pot stays dry; the plant roots stay in the inner pot. No kiln required.

items.name: Three 7 cm nursery pots
items.substitution: To use as sizing reference during building

content.text: The nursery pot acts as a sizing guide throughout the build: you are building a pot that the inner terracotta pot can drop into. Hold the nursery pot inside the build periodically to check the diameter.
Press 25 g of clay into a disc about 8 cm across and 8 mm thick. This is wider than the nursery pot base so the pot sits inside comfortably.

Roll four coils about 10 mm thick and 25 cm long. Score the base rim and slurry it, then place the first coil around the base edge and blend the inside seam downward with a finger. Add three more coils the same way. This produces a pot about 10 cm tall. Blend each interior seam before placing the next coil.

While the walls are still soft: press a stamp or fabric square into the exterior wall of one pot; use a needle tool to incise simple parallel lines on the second; leave the third plain and smooth the exterior with a wooden rib. Three different surfaces, one consistent shape.

Drop the nursery pot into the clay pot while the clay is still soft. The rim of the nursery pot should sit at the clay rim or 5 mm below. If too tight, gently press the clay walls outward while still soft. If too loose, add a thin strip of clay inside the top to act as a ledge.
Leave the pots on the board, covered loosely with plastic until

, then remove the plastic and allow them to finish drying open. Full drying takes two days. Once bone-dry, seal inside and outside with PVA or acrylic varnish. Remove the nursery pot before sealing the inside, then replace when dry.
Keep the outer clay pot dry. Water the plant through the inner nursery pot only. The outer pot is for display; the inner pot handles drainage and watering.

items.fix: Cut the base discs from a template: a circle cut from card to the correct diameter
items.cause: Base discs were rolled to different diameters

items.fix: While still soft, use a wooden rib on the inside to open the diameter slightly
items.cause: Coils were placed at a slight inward angle
items.symptom: Nursery pot does not drop in cleanly
